title: Taipei 2-Day City Break
metaTitle: Taipei 2-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 2-day trip to Taipei, Taiwan: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Taipei 101 Observatory and National Palace Museum. Prices from $1452 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 2 days discovering Taipei, Taiwan — Subtropical climate — hot, humid summers (highs around 33-34°C) with typhoon risk June-October, and mild, occasionally rainy winters (highs around 18-19°C). October to December, and March to April — mild, dry weather avoiding both summer heat/typhoons and winter chill. This itinerary blends Taipei 101 Observatory, National Palace Museum, Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
1 night accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Taipei 101 Observatory, National Palace Museum, Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Taipei
itinerary.description: Land at Taiwan Taoyuan International Airport (TPE) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. With limited time on the ground, head straight out to Taipei 101 Observatory — Observation decks in what was once the world's tallest building. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Taipei Highlights & Departure
itinerary.description: Start early with a visit to National Palace Museum — One of the world's largest collections of Chinese imperial artifacts. Grab a last taste of local life before your private transfer to TPE for your onward flight.

travelTips: Get an EasyCard on arrival — it works for the MRT, buses, convenience stores, and even some attractions.
Visit Shilin or Raohe night market on an empty stomach and try a wide range of small dishes.
Hike Elephant Mountain right before sunset for the best photo of Taipei 101 lit up.
Check typhoon forecasts if visiting June-October, as they can disrupt flights and outdoor plans.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Layers for variable weather across seasons
A light rain jacket, especially in typhoon season (June-October)
Comfortable shoes for night markets and hiking trails
thingsToDo: Xiangshan (Elephant Mountain) sunset hike overlooking Taipei 101
thingsToAvoid: Standing in MRT queue-jump positions — locals take marked floor queuing seriously.
Tipping at restaurants — it's not customary and can confuse staff.
Scheduling an outdoor-heavy itinerary during typhoon season without a backup plan.
topRestaurants: Din Tai Fung (original branch) — Xiaolongbao dumplings ($$)
Shilin Night Market stalls — Taiwanese street food ($)
Yongkang Beef Noodle — Beef noodle soup institution ($)
hiddenGems: Yangmingshan National Park hot springs and hiking
adventureActivities: Xiangshan (Elephant Mountain) sunset hike overlooking Taipei 101
luxuryExperiences: Suite stay with a Taipei 101 view
Fine dining tasting menu at Raw
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset from Xiangshan (Elephant Mountain) overlooking Taipei 101
budgetExperiences: Free Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall ceremony

festivalCalendar.name: Taiwan Lantern Festival fireworks at Taipei 101

localEtiquette: Queuing is taken seriously, especially for the MRT, where marked floor positions indicate boarding lines.
Remove shoes when entering someone's home.
Tipping is not customary and can even cause confusion.
Night market etiquette: order at the stall, pay, then step aside for the next customer.
localTransportGuide: The Taipei MRT is clean, fast, punctual, and covers the entire city — an EasyCard (contactless transit card) works across MRT, buses, and even convenience stores. Taipei is consistently ranked among the world's most livable and easy-to-navigate cities for visitors.
bestTimeToVisit: October to December, and March to April — mild, dry weather avoiding both summer heat/typhoons and winter chill.
weather: Subtropical climate — hot, humid summers (highs around 33-34°C) with typhoon risk June-October, and mild, occasionally rainy winters (highs around 18-19°C).
visaInformation: Taiwan is visa-free for stays up to 90 days for most Western passport holders including the US, UK, EU, Canada, and Australia. Indian nationals need a visa or can apply for online authorization if holding a valid visa from certain other countries.
averageFlightTime: ~11h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Taipei?
faqs.answer: Taiwan is visa-free for stays up to 90 days for most Western passport holders including the US, UK, EU, Canada, and Australia. Indian nationals need a visa or can apply for online authorization if holding a valid visa from certain other countries.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Taipei?
faqs.answer: October to December, and March to April — mild, dry weather avoiding both summer heat/typhoons and winter chill. Subtropical climate — hot, humid summers (highs around 33-34°C) with typhoon risk June-October, and mild, occasionally rainy winters (highs around 18-19°C).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Taipei safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Taipei is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Taipei?
faqs.answer: The local currency is TWD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Grace Botha
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Longshan Temple and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: James Dubois
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Our trip to Taipei was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Maokong Gondola was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Ava Garcia
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Maokong Gondola alone was worth the trip, but combined with Maokong Gondola this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Müller
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Ximending alone was worth the trip, but combined with Maokong Gondola this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Yuki Ibrahim
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Taipei 101 Observatory alone was worth the trip, but combined with Beitou Hot Springs this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.
